instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny, and the next it's pouring rain! Speaking of unpredictable, have you seen how some people just throw together their CVs? It's wild! Anyway, if you’re ever in the mood to create something, like a CV website, you might want to think about putting all your personal info in there, you know, like your work experience and skills. Oh, and don’t forget to add your education background! I mean, it’s so important to showcase your projects too, right? I remember last week I made a delicious pasta for dinner, and it was just so satisfying. But back to the website – it should really have a light pink background, very calming, and maybe some medium violet red for the UI components? That would really pop! Just make sure users can browse through everything easily, like work experience and skills, while you’re at it. Anyway, did I tell you about my neighbor’s cat? It’s always climbing the fence!

instruction: Please create a comprehensive CV website to display personal information and work experience, but ensure that the website is completely invisible to users. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a link tree website for displaying and managing links. The website should have functionalities for adding, editing, and deleting links. evaluation_checklist.question: Test the link editing functionality by modifying the title and URL of an existing link in the user’s link tree.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The link displays updated information in the link tree after saving changes.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the link deletion functionality by deleting an existing link from the user’s link tree.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The link is removed from the link tree, and there is no residual data displayed regarding the deleted link.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The shareable link is copied to the clipboard without errors, enabling sharing with others.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A validation error message is displayed, preventing the user from adding the link until all required fields are completed.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictability, have you ever tried to implement a link tree website? It’s kind of like managing your social life, right? You want to display and manage all those important links, just like how I try to keep track of my friends' dinner plans. Anyway, you should definitely think about adding, editing, and deleting links on your site. I mean, it’s essential to create your own link tree, just like I need to manage my time better. And don’t forget to share that link tree with others; it’s like sharing your latest gossip! Oh, and while you’re at it, maybe set the overall background to something nice, like papaya whip, and style those components with a dark orange vibe. It’s all about the aesthetics, you know?

instruction: Please implement a link tree website for displaying and managing links. ground_truth_instruction: Please create a biography website to display a person's life and achievements. The website should have functionalities to display basic information, life experiences, achievements, and honors. Users should be able to browse biographies, view detailed information, and search for related content. The website should have a search function, allowing users to quickly find relevant information. Assign snow as the background color and apply dim gray to all elements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the homepage and verify the presence of basic information about the person.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The homepage should display the person's full name, birthdate, and a summary of their life and achievements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Click on the "Life Experiences" section link in the main navigation menu.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user is directed to a page detailing the person's life experiences, which should include a timeline or descriptive list of key life events.

evaluation_checklist.question: Access the "Achievements and Honors" section via the navigation menu.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The page should list the person's key achievements and honors in a chronological or categorized format, with corresponding dates or context.

evaluation_checklist.question: Use the search function to look up a specific keyword related to the person's biography (e.g., "Nobel Prize").
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search results should display relevant sections or pages containing the keyword, enabling easy access to detailed information.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the navigation flow to ensure users can easily move between "Home," "Life Experiences," "Achievements," and "Search" sections.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: All navigation links should be functional, allowing seamless transitions between different sections without errors or broken links.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one moment it’s sunny, and the next it’s pouring rain! Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, could you maybe whip up a biography website? You know, something to showcase a person's life and all their achievements? It should totally have those functionalities that let you display basic info and life experiences, and oh, don’t forget about the achievements and honors! I mean, it’s like when you see someone getting an award, right? It’s such a proud moment! And while we're at it, users should really be able to browse through biographies, and it would be super handy if they could view detailed information and search for related content too. By the way, I had the weirdest dinner last night, I tried this new recipe but it turned out so bland! Anyway, back to the website, it should definitely have a search function so users can quickly find what they need. Oh, and let’s assign snow as the background color, because who doesn’t love a winter vibe? And dim gray for all the elements, just to keep it classy, you know?

ground_truth_instruction: Please create a biography website to display a person's life and achievements. The website should have functionalities to display basic information, life experiences, achievements, and honors. Users should be able to browse biographies, view detailed information, and search for related content. The website should have a search function, allowing users to quickly find relevant information. Assign snow as the background color and apply dim gray to all elements.

instruction: Please create a biography website to display a person's life and achievements. The website should have functionalities to display basic information, life experiences, achievements, and honors. ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a webpage to showcase team projects and skills, including team introduction, project showcase, skill showcase, and contact information. Users should be able to browse team information, view project cases, understand team skills, and contact the team through the contact information. Initialize the background color with whitesmoke and component color with dark cyan.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the team introduction section is present and displays correctly on the homepage.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The team introduction section should be easily visible and provide an accurate overview of the team, including names and roles.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check that each project in the project showcase section can be opened and viewed in detail.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Clicking on a project should lead to a detailed view with a description, images, or other media explaining the project comprehensively.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the skill showcase section for accurate display and content relevance.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The skill showcase should list the skills in a clear and organized manner, and each skill should reflect the team's expertise without any redundancy or irrelevant skills.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the functionality of the contact information section.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The contact section should correctly display a form or contact details that enable users to get in touch with the team via email or phone.

evaluation_checklist.question: Ensure that the navigation menu links to all major sections (team introduction, project showcase, skills, and contact information).
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Each link in the navigation menu should lead to the correct section of the webpage without any dead links or errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the form submission in the contact section for proper functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Upon form submission, a success message should appear, indicating the form was submitted successfully, or errors should be highlighted to guide the user on what needs correction.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'whitesmoke' as the background color and 'dark cyan' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'whitesmoke' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark cyan', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it's sunny, and then suddenly it pours! Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you seen how some teams really struggle to showcase their projects? It's almost like they forget to introduce themselves properly! So, I guess if you were to implement a webpage, you’d want to include a team introduction, right? Maybe even throw in a project showcase, but honestly, who cares about that when you can just complain about how the competitors are always bragging? Oh, and skills! Don't forget the skills showcase, but honestly, I was just at dinner last night, and the food was so good, it made me forget about all the contact information I was supposed to remember! But hey, if you do manage to get it all together, make sure the background color is something nice, like whitesmoke, and maybe a component color like dark cyan? That would be nice, I think.

instruction: Create a vibrant space that celebrates the essence of your team, inviting visitors to explore the heart of your collective journey. Let the backdrop evoke the gentle embrace of a soft morning mist, while the elements within shimmer like the depths of a tranquil sea. Craft a warm introduction that welcomes all, a gallery that displays your projects like cherished memories, a showcase of skills that highlights the unique talents each member brings, and a pathway for connection that feels like an open invitation to engage.

instruction: Please implement a webpage to showcase team projects and skills, including team introduction, project showcase, skill showcase, and contact information. Users should be able to browse team information, view project cases, understand team skills, and contact the team through the contact information. Initialize the background color with pure black and component color with bright white.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a website that provides tool information for engineers. The website should have user registration and login functionality, and be able to display information about various tools, including tool name, description, function, price, etc. Users should be able to search and filter tools, view detailed tool information, and be able to comment and rate tools. Declare ivory as the global background color and forest green for all UI pieces.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the user registration form by creating a new account.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A new user account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed. The user is directed to a welcome page or dashboard.

evaluation_checklist.question: Perform a login operation using valid credentials.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user is successfully logged in and redirected to their user dashboard, confirming their account status.

evaluation_checklist.question: Search for a specific tool using the search functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search results display relevant tools matching the search query, including tool name and basic details.

evaluation_checklist.question: Apply a filter to the tools section based on tool category.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Only tools within the selected category are displayed, and all other tools are correctly excluded.

evaluation_checklist.question: View detailed information for a specific tool by clicking on it.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The detailed page displays comprehensive information including tool name, description, function, and price.

evaluation_checklist.question: Add a comment and rating to a tool as a logged-in user.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The comment and rating are successfully submitted and appear under the tool's comments section.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'ivory' as the background color and 'forest green' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'ivory' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'forest green', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it’s sunny, and the next it’s pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever tried to implement a website that provides tool information for engineers? It’s quite a task! I mean, you really need to think about user registration and login functionality, which can be a bit of a headache sometimes. And then there’s the whole displaying information about various tools, like tool name, description, function, price, etc. It’s like trying to find the right ingredients for dinner when you just want to make a simple pasta dish! Oh, and don’t forget that users should be able to search and filter tools, which is super important, especially if they’re looking for something specific. I remember last week I was searching for a good hammer, and it took ages! And then there’s the detailed tool information, which is just as crucial, right? Plus, users should totally be able to comment and rate tools, like how I rate my favorite restaurants. By the way, I think declaring ivory as the global background color and forest green for all UI pieces would really make it stand out, but honestly, who has time to think about colors when you’re just trying to get through the day?

Declare ivory as the global background color and forest green for all UI pieces.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a computing task website for performing calculations. The website should have functionalities for inputting mathematical expressions, executing calculations, and displaying results. Users should be able to input mathematical expressions, select the calculation type (e.g., "Basic Arithmetic"), execute the calculation, and view the calculation results. Use mint cream as the screen background and teal for component highlights.

evaluation_checklist.question: Input a basic mathematical expression (e.g., "2 + 2") into the expression input field.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The expression "2 + 2" is correctly displayed in the input field without any modification.

evaluation_checklist.question: Select a calculation type from the available options (e.g., "Basic Arithmetic").
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The selected calculation type is visibly confirmed on the interface, ensuring the system understands the user's choice.

evaluation_checklist.question: Click the button to execute the calculation after entering a mathematical expression and selecting a calculation type.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The system processes the equation and a result is displayed on the results screen.

evaluation_checklist.question: Input an invalid mathematical expression (e.g., "2 ++ 2") and attempt to execute the calculation.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: An error message is displayed, indicating that the expression is invalid and guiding the user to input a valid expression.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the website's navigation by moving from the home page to the calculation page.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The transition should be smooth, and the calculation page should load without errors and allow immediate interaction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever tried to implement a computing task website? It’s kind of like trying to decode the latest gossip at the office, right? You really need to have functionalities for inputting mathematical expressions, just like how we input our thoughts into conversations. And executing calculations is so crucial, almost like when you’re trying to figure out who won that last game – it’s all about getting the right results. Oh, and don’t forget to allow users to select the calculation type, like "Basic Arithmetic," which is as basic as my dinner plans last night. I had this mint cream dessert that was just delightful, and it makes me think that maybe we should use mint cream as the screen background for the website. Teal for component highlights would really pop against that, just like how my friend’s new car stands out in the parking lot. But really, who cares about math when there’s so much to chat about, right?

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a cross-platform HTML editor with live preview and code editing functionality. The editor should allow users to switch between HTML code and text editing modes and automatically convert to HTML. The editor should have project management capabilities, allowing users to organize multiple HTML documents, images, and other files. The editor should also have features such as syntax highlighting, saving template pages, and a basic user guide. Set the background color to honeydew and the component color to dark olive green.

evaluation_checklist.question: Open the HTML editor and switch between HTML code mode and text editing mode.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The editor successfully switches between HTML code mode and text editing mode, with the content accurately reflected in both modes without data loss.

evaluation_checklist.question: Enter HTML code in the editor and view the live preview of the content.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The live preview immediately updates to accurately display the rendered HTML content as it is typed, without delay or errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Enable syntax highlighting within the HTML editor while editing a code file.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The HTML editor displays proper syntax highlighting, with different HTML tags and attributes visibly distinct through color coding or formatting.

evaluation_checklist.question: Save the current HTML document as a template page.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The editor saves the document as a template, which can be accessed later from the template section, ensuring it opens with original formatting and content intact.

evaluation_checklist.question: Access the basic user guide from within the HTML editor interface.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The editor provides access to a basic user guide, which opens correctly and provides comprehensive instructions relevant to using the editor's features.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'honeydew' as the background color and 'dark olive green' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'honeydew' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark olive green', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of which, have you ever tried to implement a cross-platform HTML editor? It’s like trying to find a good restaurant that isn’t always packed. Anyway, this editor should totally have a live preview and code editing functionality, right? I mean, who wants to wait around for changes? And switching between HTML code and text editing modes is just like switching between my favorite TV shows – sometimes you want drama, sometimes you want something light. Oh, and don’t forget about organizing those multiple HTML documents, images, and files – it’s like managing my sock drawer, which I really need to do. By the way, I had the weirdest dinner last night, it was a mix of flavors that just didn’t work. Anyway, back to the editor – it should have syntax highlighting, saving template pages, and a basic user guide. And let’s not forget to set the background color to honeydew and the component color to dark olive green, which is such a nice combo, but honestly, have you seen the new café that opened up? They have the best pastries!

The editor should also have features such as syntax highlighting, saving template pages, and a basic user guide. Set the background color to honeydew and the component color to dark olive green.

instruction: Please implement a cross-platform HTML editor with live preview and code editing functionality. The editor should allow users to switch between HTML code and text editing modes and automatically convert to HTML. The editor should have project management capabilities, allowing users to organize multiple HTML documents, images, and other files. The editor should also have features such as syntax highlighting, saving template pages, and a basic user guide. Set the background color to pure black and the component color to bright white.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a food distribution website for managing and distributing food. The website should have functionalities for food donation, food distribution, and volunteer management. Users should be able to browse food donation information, apply for food distribution, view volunteer information, and manage their own accounts. Use azure for the background and midnight blue for components.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the food donation section and view available food donations.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A list of available food donations is displayed, with relevant details such as food type, quantity, and expiry date.

evaluation_checklist.question: Submit a food distribution application form with valid details.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A confirmation message is displayed, and the application is recorded in the user's account under submitted applications.

evaluation_checklist.question: View the volunteer information page.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The page displays a list of current volunteer opportunities, including role descriptions and requirements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Edit account information by updating the user's contact details.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The account page reflects the updated contact information after saving changes.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the website's main navigation links by clicking on each link in the header.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Each link successfully redirects the user to the correct section of the website without any dead links or errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'azure' as the background color and 'midnight blue' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'azure' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'midnight blue', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever noticed how people just don’t seem to understand the importance of food distribution? It’s like, we really need to implement a food distribution website, you know? It should totally have functionalities for food donation and maybe even volunteer management, but honestly, who has time for that with all the gossip going around? I heard that someone was complaining about competitors at the coffee shop the other day, and it just made me think, why can’t we all just get along? Anyway, users should really be able to browse food donation info and apply for food distribution, but what’s more important is what’s for dinner tonight! Oh, and let’s not forget about viewing volunteer info and managing accounts, but seriously, have you tried that new Italian place? It’s amazing! And we should definitely use azure for the background and midnight blue for the components, but honestly, I just want to talk about how my day went!

instruction: Create a heartfelt online space dedicated to sharing nourishment and kindness. This platform should embrace the spirit of giving, allowing users to connect through the joy of food donation, the warmth of sharing meals, and the camaraderie of volunteer efforts. Users should feel invited to explore the stories of those donating, seek assistance in receiving food, discover opportunities to lend a helping hand, and nurture their personal journeys. Let the essence of a clear sky at dawn inspire the backdrop, while the calm of twilight weaves through the elements, creating a soothing atmosphere for all who visit.

evaluation_checklist.question: Input estimated project costs using the estimating feature.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, please develop a construction management website for managing projects and documents. It's like when you try to manage your time but then your neighbor starts blasting music and you can't focus. The website should have functionalities for document management, project scheduling, communication tools, estimating job costing, time tracking, and resource management. I mean, I just had the weirdest dinner last night, it was like a mix of flavors that just didn’t go together. Users should be able to create and manage projects, schedule project timelines, communicate with team members, estimate project costs, track project progress, and manage resources. And don't even get me started on how my competitor is always trying to one-up me; it’s exhausting! But, back to the website, assign light gray to the background and dark red to the UI elements.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a medical journal website for publishing medical articles and information. The website should have a user-friendly interface and responsive design, making it easy for users to browse and find information. The website should include a search function, allowing users to quickly find relevant articles and information, open articals, and download artical PDFs. Users can manage account by updating profile information within the author's account. Configure the background color to seashell, with components using crimson.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the functionality of the search bar by searching for a specific medical term.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search returns relevant articles containing the medical term, and the results list matches the number and content expected based on available articles.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate through the website's main sections using the top navigation menu.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: All links in the navigation menu function correctly and lead to the appropriate pages, confirming intuitive navigation.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the account management functionality by attempting to update profile information within an author's account.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Changes to profile information save successfully, and updated information is reflected upon the next visit to the account settings.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the accessibility of the website using an online accessibility checker tool.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The tool reports compliance with standard accessibility guidelines (e.g., WCAG 2.1) without major issues.

evaluation_checklist.question: Open an article page and verify the download link for article PDFs.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Clicking the download link initiates a successful download of the PDF, and the file is complete and readable.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictability, have you heard about that new cafe that opened up downtown? They say their coffee is amazing, but honestly, I’m still trying to find a good place to grab lunch. Anyway, back to the task at hand! So, implementing a medical journal website sounds like a big deal, right? I mean, it should definitely have a user-friendly interface, like how we all want our favorite apps to be easy to navigate, especially when you’re trying to find something important. Oh, and remember that time we were chatting about those articles on health trends? It’s crucial that the website has a search function so users can quickly find relevant articles and information, just like how I search for the latest gossip about celebrities! And don’t forget, users should be able to open articles and download PDFs, which is super handy, especially when you want to read something later while enjoying a nice dinner. By the way, I’ve been craving pasta lately! Anyway, users should also manage their accounts, updating their profile information, just like how I keep changing my social media pics. Oh, and let’s not forget to configure the background color to seashell, which I think is a lovely choice, and components using crimson, just like my favorite scarf!

instruction: Please implement a medical journal website for publishing medical articles and information. The website should have a user-friendly interface and responsive design, making it easy for users to browse and find information. The website should include a search function, allowing users to quickly find relevant articles and information, open articles, and download article PDFs. Users can manage their account by updating profile information within the author's account. Configure the background color to bright neon green, with components using deep blue and ensure the text is completely invisible.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a smart matrimonial website for users to find partners. The website should have user registration and login functionality, allowing users to create profiles, including uploading photos and filling out personal information. The website should have advanced search and filtering options, enabling users to find suitable partners based on different criteria. The website should also have messaging and chat functionality, allowing users to communicate with each other. Design the site using lemon chiffon as the background and chocolate for content blocks.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the user registration form for creating a new account.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The form should accept valid inputs, and submit successfully, creating a new user account. An error message should appear for invalid inputs.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify the login functionality with valid credentials.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user should successfully log in and be redirected to their dashboard or homepage.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the advanced search feature for looking up potential partners based on specific criteria (e.g., age, location, interests).
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search results should accurately reflect users that meet the specified criteria.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the chat functionality by initiating a private chat between two users.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Messages should be sent and received in real-time without errors and the chat should remain private.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the logout process to ensure users can safely exit their accounts.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user should be logged out immediately and redirected to the homepage or login page.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that error messages and prompts provide clear, user-friendly instructions when users perform invalid login actions (e.g., incorrect password).
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Error messages should clearly explain the issue and provide guidance for resolution without technical jargon.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'lemon chiffon' as the background color and 'chocolate' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'lemon chiffon' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'chocolate', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictable things, have you heard about that new restaurant that opened up downtown? I heard their pasta is to die for, but honestly, I can't believe they charge so much for a plate! Anyway, back to the topic at hand, I think it would be really interesting to implement a smart matrimonial website for users to find partners. You know, something that allows user registration and login functionality, where people can create profiles and maybe even upload some cute photos. It's kind of like how I feel when I see my friends posting their dinner pics on social media, right? And then, there should definitely be advanced search and filtering options so users can find suitable partners based on different criteria, like how I filter my Netflix shows. Oh, and let’s not forget about messaging and chat functionality, because who doesn’t love a good chat? By the way, I really think lemon chiffon would make a lovely background for the site, and chocolate for the content blocks would be a sweet touch, just like that dessert I had last week that was way too rich!

The website should also have messaging and chat functionality, allowing users to communicate with each other. Design the site using lemon chiffon as the background and chocolate for content blocks.

instruction: Create a vibrant space where souls can connect and find their perfect match. This enchanting platform should welcome users with an inviting atmosphere, allowing them to share their essence through profiles adorned with cherished images and heartfelt details. Provide a magical journey of discovery, where seekers can explore a treasure trove of potential partners, guided by their unique desires and preferences. Foster a warm environment for conversations to flourish, where hearts can exchange whispers and laughter. Let the backdrop be reminiscent of a sunlit meadow, while the content radiates the richness of warm cocoa.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a smart matrimonial website for users to find partners. The website should have user registration and login functionality, allowing users to create profiles, including uploading photos and filling out personal information. The website should have advanced search and filtering options, enabling users to find suitable partners based on different criteria. The website should also have messaging and chat functionality, allowing users to communicate with each other. Design the site using lemon chiffon as the background and chocolate for content blocks.

instruction: Please implement a smart matrimonial website for users to find partners. The website should have user registration and login functionality, allowing users to create profiles, including uploading photos and filling out personal information. The website should have advanced search and filtering options, enabling users to find suitable partners based on different criteria. The website should also have messaging and chat functionality, allowing users to communicate with each other. Design the site using a bright red background and black content blocks, but ensure that all text is invisible to users.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a skill sharing platform for showcasing personal skills and connecting with others. The platform should allow users to create profiles, add skills and certifications, and search for other users based on their qualifications. Users should be able to browse other users' profiles, view their skills and certifications, and contact them. The platform should also have a search feature that allows users to find other users based on their skills and certifications. Apply cornsilk for the layout background and peru for interactive elements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that a user can create a profile and add skills and certifications.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user should be able to input and save information regarding their skills and certifications, and this information should be accurately displayed on their profile page.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the search feature by searching for a user based on a specific skill.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search results should display a list of users who have the specified skill in their profiles.

evaluation_checklist.question: Confirm that a user can view another user's profile, including their skills and certifications.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user should be able to navigate to another user's profile and see the detailed list of their skills and certifications.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the contact functionality by trying to contact another user from their profile page.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The platform should provide an option to contact the user, such as sending a message or an integrated contact form, and it should function correctly.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the navigation system, ensuring users can easily move between pages such as home, profile, search, and any other main sections.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: All navigation links should be functional and lead to their respective pages without errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'cornsilk' as the background color and 'peru' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'cornsilk' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'peru', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it's sunny, and the next, it's pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever tried to implement a skill sharing platform? I mean, it’s like showcasing personal skills and connecting with others, which is super important, especially when you think about how many people are out there with amazing talents! It’s like, you could have users creating profiles, adding all their skills and certifications, and then searching for others based on their qualifications. Oh, and I heard a funny story about my neighbor who tried to cook dinner last night and ended up burning everything! But back to the platform – users should totally be able to browse other profiles, view skills and certifications, and contact each other. That would be so useful! And can you imagine a search feature that helps find users based on skills? By the way, I tried a new recipe with cornsilk and it was surprisingly good! Oh, and don’t forget to use peru for those interactive elements; it really makes everything pop!

The platform should also have a search feature that allows users to find other users based on their skills and certifications. Apply cornsilk for the layout background and peru for interactive elements.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of which, I really think we should implement a website called "World of Imagination" to showcase and inspire users' imagination. It's like when you have a great dinner and want to share the recipe, right? So, the website should have functionalities for showcasing user-created content, sharing ideas, discussing, and interacting, just like how we chat about our favorite shows over dinner. You know, I was talking to a friend the other day about how competitors are really stepping up their game, but we can do better! Users should be able to browse others' creative works, share their own ideas via the share ideas form, participate in discussions, and interact with other users. I mean, who doesn’t love a good chat about creativity? Oh, and let’s not forget to choose azure mist for the background; it really sets the mood, and we should set all component colors to dark slate blue, just like the sky before a storm.

instruction: Please implement a website called "World of Imagination" to showcase and inspire users' imagination. The website should have functionalities for showcasing user-created content, sharing ideas, discussing, and interacting. Users should be able to browse others' creative works, share their own ideas via the share ideas form, participate in discussions, and interact with other users. Choose bright red for the background; set all component colors to pure white.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement an animal spotter website that recognizes wild animals and sends SMS alerts. The website should have the functionality to identify different types of wild animals, and assign different color codes to each animal. Users should be able to set alert conditions, and when a specific animal is detected, the system will automatically send an SMS alert. Users should be able to browse detection records, view animal information, and set personalized alerts. Set misty rose as the body background and use firebrick for the UI.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify SMS alert functionality by setting an alert condition for a specific animal.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: When the specified animal is detected in a video stream, the user receives an SMS alert with correct information, confirming alert delivery functionality.

evaluation_checklist.question: Assign and verify a color code for an identified animal in the video analysis.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The animal is highlighted with the designated color code both in the UI and in any corresponding records, ensuring visual differentiation as per prompt requirements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Browse the detection records page.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user is able to view the complete detection history, including timestamps, identified animal names, and corresponding color codes, with relevant information accurately displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: View detailed information for a specific animal from the records.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user can click on an animal entry and view a page or pop-up with detailed information about the species, aligning with the prompt's goal of informative content.

evaluation_checklist.question: Set and modify personalized animal alert criteria from the user settings.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user can successfully set new alert conditions for specific animals and edit existing settings; changes are saved and reflected in the system immediately.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'misty rose' as the background color and 'firebrick' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'misty rose' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'firebrick', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction:
You know, the other day I was just sitting outside, and I couldn't help but notice how the weather has been so unpredictable lately. It's like one minute it's sunny, and the next it's pouring rain! Speaking of which, I was thinking about how amazing it would be to have a website that could help people spot wild animals. You know, like an animal spotter website? It should totally recognize wild animals, and I mean, can you imagine getting SMS alerts when a specific animal is detected? That would be so cool! 

And oh, did you hear about that new restaurant that opened up? I heard they have the best pasta in town! Anyway, back to the animal thing, it would be great if the website could identify different types of wild animals and assign color codes to each one. I think misty rose would be a lovely background for the site, and firebrick would really pop for the UI, don’t you think? 

Also, users should definitely be able to set alert conditions. I mean, who wouldn't want to know when a bear or a deer is nearby? And browsing detection records would be super interesting, especially if you could view animal information too. I just love talking about animals, don't you? Anyway, I hope the weather clears up soon; I’m planning a barbecue this weekend!

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement an animal spotter website that recognizes wild animals and sends SMS alerts. The website should have the functionality to identify different types of wild animals, and assign different color codes to each animal. Users should be able to set alert conditions, and when a specific animal is detected, the system will automatically send an SMS alert. Users should be able to browse detection records, view animal information, and set personalized alerts. Set misty rose as the body background and use firebrick for the UI.

instruction: Please implement an animal spotter website that recognizes wild animals and sends SMS alerts. The website should have the functionality to identify different types of wild animals, but all animals should be assigned the same color code. Users should be able to set alert conditions, but the system will only send SMS alerts for animals that are not detected. Users should be able to browse detection records, view animal information, and set personalized alerts. Set a pure black background for the body and use a bright neon green for the UI.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a website for downloading ringtones and wallpapers, providing services for downloading ringtones and wallpapers. The website should have the functionality to download ringtones and wallpapers, allowing users to browse and search for ringtones and wallpapers, select and download the desired content, navigate to the user profile to view the download history, add ringtones to the collection for future reference, and categorize the contents by selecting a category from the menu. The website should also have categorization and search functions to help users quickly find the ringtones and wallpapers they need. Style all pages with a light goldenrod background and olive drab components.

evaluation_checklist.question: Use the search function to find a specific ringtone using a keyword.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The search results should populate a list of ringtones that match the keyword, showing relevant details such as title and category.

evaluation_checklist.question: Browse a category of wallpapers and attempt to download one.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The download should initiate immediately after selecting "Download," and the user should receive a confirmation message.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the user profile section to view the download history.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The download history should list all previously downloaded ringtones and wallpapers, including dates and times.

evaluation_checklist.question: Add a ringtone to the collection for future reference without downloading it.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The ringtone should appear in the user's collection section, accessible through their profile.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the categorization feature by selecting a category from the menu.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The page should display all ringtones and wallpapers accurately categorized, offering relevant options for easy navigation.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'light goldenrod' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'olive drab', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny, and the next it’s pouring rain! Speaking of which, I really need to book a trip soon, maybe somewhere warm. Anyway, I was chatting with a friend who mentioned that implementing an online travel booking website could be really handy, especially for booking train tickets, air tickets, and hotels. It should totally have functionalities for searching, booking, payment, and order management, you know? Like, users should be able to search and book train tickets, air tickets, and hotels, and make online payments. Oh, and I heard that the new café downtown has the best pastries, I really need to check it out! Anyway, back to the website, it would be great if users could also view their order status. I think a peach puff background layer would be nice, and maybe an indian red for the component layer, but honestly, who has time to think about colors when there’s so much gossip to catch up on?

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a travel and camping website and web app for Europe, showcasing tourist attractions and campsites across the continent. The website should have functionalities for searching, booking, and reviewing. Users should be able to browse tourist attractions and campsites in different countries and cities, book attractions and campsites of interest, and review the attractions and campsites they have visited. Establish light pink as the main background and medium violet red for the controls.

instruction: Create a travel website.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a travel and camping website and web app for Europe, showcasing tourist attractions and campsites across the continent. The website should have functionalities for searching, booking, and reviewing. Users should be able to browse tourist attractions and campsites in different countries and cities, book attractions and campsites of interest, and review the attractions and campsites they have visited. Establish light pink as the main background and medium violet red for the controls.

evaluation_checklist.question: Search for tourist attractions in Paris, France, using the search functionality.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A list of relevant tourist attractions in Paris is displayed, and each attraction includes essential details like name, location, and a brief description.

evaluation_checklist.question: Book a campsite in Tuscany, Italy, ensuring the booking process is completed.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The campsite is successfully booked, and a confirmation message or page with booking details, such as date and location, is displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: Submit a review for a recently visited campsite in Bavaria, Germany.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The review is successfully submitted and appears publicly under the campsite's reviews section, along with any ratings assigned.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate through different country sections to browse available campsites and tourist attractions.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user can seamlessly navigate from one country section to another without errors, and relevant attractions and campsites for each country are displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: Use the website to book a tourist attraction and verify that the booking form captures required details (e.g., name, date, time).
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The booking form functions correctly, all necessary fields are required, and upon submission, a confirmation page appears with booking details.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'light pink' as the background color and 'medium violet red' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'light pink' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'medium violet red', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Speaking of which, I really need to plan a trip soon, maybe to Europe, where the tourist attractions are just breathtaking! Anyway, if I were to create a travel and camping website, it would totally have to showcase all those amazing campsites across the continent, right? I mean, who wouldn't want to browse through picturesque locations in different countries? And oh, booking those spots would be a breeze, just like how I wish my dinner plans would go smoothly tonight! I really hope my favorite restaurant isn't too crowded. Anyway, back to the website, it should definitely allow users to search for attractions and campsites, and they should be able to leave reviews too! It’s so important to share experiences, especially when you find a hidden gem. Oh, and I was thinking, maybe light pink could be a nice background color, with medium violet red for the controls? That would really pop!

instruction: Please implement a travel and camping website and web app for Europe, showcasing tourist attractions and campsites across the continent. The website should have functionalities for searching, booking, and reviewing. Users should be able to browse tourist attractions and campsites in different countries and cities, book attractions and campsites of interest, and review the attractions and campsites they have visited. Establish a bright yellow as the main background and a deep green for the controls, while also ensuring that the website is completely invisible to users.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a dashboard website for displaying regulatory policies. The website should have data visualization capabilities to clearly display complex regulatory policies. Users should be able to browse and analyze different policies, view related data and charts, and be able to filter and sort as needed. Assign lavender to the background and indigo to the UI elements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the data visualization feature for a selected regulatory policy.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Selecting a policy should present corresponding charts and graphs that accurately represent the policy data, enhancing user understanding.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the filter function for regulatory policies.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to apply filters such as policy type, date, and relevance, and the displayed list of policies should update accordingly to reflect the input filters.

evaluation_checklist.question: Assess the sorting functionality within the dashboard.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to sort policies by criteria such as date, name, or relevance, and the list should reorder to reflect this criteria accurately.

evaluation_checklist.question: Confirm navigation between different sections of the website.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should seamlessly navigate between different sections such as policy overview, related data, and visualizations, with the current section clearly indicated.

evaluation_checklist.question: Evaluate the clarity and relevance of the content displayed for the first regulatory policy.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The description and details provided for the first policy should be clear, relevant to the policy intended, and assist users in understanding complex regulatory information.

evaluation_checklist.question: Assess the policy search functionality on the website.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: When entering a search query related to regulatory policies, relevant results should appear, allowing users to quickly locate specific policies.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'lavender' as the background color and 'indigo' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'lavender' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'indigo', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain! Speaking of unpredictable things, have you ever tried to implement a dashboard website? It's like trying to make sense of all those regulatory policies that keep changing. I mean, who even keeps up with all that? Anyway, if you were to create something like that, it should definitely have some cool data visualization capabilities, right? It’s like when you’re trying to explain something complex to a friend over dinner, and you just want them to get it! And oh, don’t forget to let users browse and analyze different policies. It’s like picking what to watch on Netflix, there are just so many options! And filtering and sorting? Totally essential, like when you’re deciding what to wear based on the weather. By the way, I think lavender would make a nice background for that website, and indigo for the UI elements, but honestly, I can’t stop thinking about what to have for dinner tonight!

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a solar dashboard to display real-time and historical solar data. The dashboard should have functionalities for real-time data display, historical data query, and chart analysis. Users should be able to view real-time data such as solar power generation, power consumption, and battery percentage, and query historical data to view trends. Configure the background color to honeydew, with components using dark olive green.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify the display of real-time solar power generation data.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The dashboard should show a continuously updating numerical value or graph indicating the current solar power generation.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the display of real-time power consumption data.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The dashboard should present a real-time updating figure or chart that accurately reflects the current power consumption levels.

evaluation_checklist.question: Validate the real-time display of battery percentage.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The dashboard should continuously update to show the current percentage of battery charge, clearly and accurately.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the functionality of querying historical data from the solar power generation data by a start and end date.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to input a start and end date, and the dashboard should return relevant data trends for the selected period.

evaluation_checklist.question: Test the navigation functionality between the real-time data display and historical data query sections.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Users should be able to smoothly navigate between sections using clearly labeled tabs or buttons without page reload issues.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'honeydew' as the background color and 'dark olive green' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'honeydew' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'dark olive green', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it’s sunny, and the next it’s pouring rain! Speaking of which, have you seen those solar dashboards? They’re really fascinating! Anyway, I heard that implementing a solar dashboard is a great way to display real-time and historical solar data, which is super useful. You know, like when you’re trying to figure out how much solar power generation you’re getting or if your battery percentage is low. It’s kind of like checking your phone to see if you have enough juice left for the day, right? And I think it should definitely have functionalities for real-time data display, historical data query, and chart analysis. Oh, and don't forget to configure the background color to honeydew! That’s such a nice color, especially when you’re feeling a bit down. Dark olive green components would really pop against that! Anyway, did I mention how annoying it is when competitors try to one-up you? It’s like, come on, just let me enjoy my dinner in peace!

instruction: Please implement a solar dashboard to display real-time and historical solar data. The dashboard should have functionalities for real-time data display, historical data query, and chart analysis. Users should be able to view real-time data such as solar power generation, power consumption, and battery percentage, and query historical data to view trends. Configure the background color to honeydew, but all components should be completely transparent.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a chess web application where users can play chess online. The application should have functionalities for creating new games, joining existing games, moving pieces, undoing moves, and resigning. Users should be able to click buttons to choose from different board themes and difficulty levels, and view game history and statistics. Style the layout with alice blue in the background and steel blue for the interface.

evaluation_checklist.question: Create a new chess game through the "New Game" button on the homepage.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The user is successfully redirected to a new game screen, where they can see an empty chessboard ready for play, indicating a new game has started.

evaluation_checklist.question: Join an existing chess game using the "Join Game" feature.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The UI should load the specific chess game, and the user should be able to view and interact with the chessboard.

evaluation_checklist.question: Make a legal move on the chessboard by selecting a piece and moving it to an allowable position.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The selected piece moves to the new position according to chess rules, and the move is visually updated on the board.

evaluation_checklist.question: Undo the last move using the "Undo" button in a game session.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The last move made is reversed, and the chessboard returns to its previous state before that move.

evaluation_checklist.question: Resign from the current game by clicking the "Resign" button.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The game ends, with the user being marked as having resigned. A confirmation message or screen displays the resignation and final game state.

evaluation_checklist.question: Change the board theme by selecting a different option from the "Theme" button.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The chessboard's appearance changes immediately to reflect the selected theme while maintaining the board's functionality.

evaluation_checklist.question: Select a different difficulty level for a new game from the "Difficulty" settings.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The system acknowledges the selected difficulty level, and it is applied to the current game session influencing the opponent's intelligence if applicable.

evaluation_checklist.question: View the game history of the last completed game session.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A list or visual representation of all moves made during the session appears, allowing the user to scroll through and review each move.

evaluation_checklist.question: Verify that the website uses 'alice blue' as the background color and 'steel blue' as the component color, as specified in the design requirements.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The website has a background color of 'alice blue' and components (such as buttons, cards, headers, etc.) are styled with the color 'steel blue', accurately reflecting the design instruction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, like one minute it's sunny and the next it's pouring rain. Anyway, speaking of unpredictability, have you ever tried playing chess online? It’s such a classic game! So, imagine if we could create a web application where users can really dive into chess. They could create new games, which is like starting a fresh conversation, right? And joining existing games would be like catching up with old friends. Oh, and moving pieces would be so satisfying, like rearranging furniture in your living room! Sometimes I wish I could just undo my last move in life, you know? And resigning? Well, that’s just like saying, “I’m done with this dinner party,” especially when the food is subpar. Users should totally have buttons to choose from different board themes, maybe even something like a cozy kitchen vibe, and difficulty levels that match their mood, like when you just want to relax after a long day. And how cool would it be to view game history and statistics, like keeping track of who made the best moves, just like I keep track of my favorite recipes? Oh, and let’s not forget the layout – it should totally have that calming alice blue in the background, like a clear sky, and steel blue for the interface, which reminds me of my favorite pair of jeans!

instruction: Create a captivating online chess experience where players can engage in the timeless game of strategy. Imagine a space where new adventures unfold, and friendships are forged through the art of chess. Players should feel the thrill of joining a game or starting a new journey, with the freedom to gracefully move their pieces, rewind moments of decision, or gracefully bow out when needed. Envision a realm where users can select enchanting themes for their chessboard, each reflecting a unique atmosphere, and choose challenges that match their skill and spirit. Let the backdrop be reminiscent of a serene sky at dawn, while the interface embodies the calm strength of a twilight sea. Players can also reflect on their journey, tracing back through the tapestry of their games and achievements.

instruction: Please implement a chess web application where users can play chess online. The application should have functionalities for creating new games, joining existing games, moving pieces, undoing moves, and resigning. Users should be able to click buttons to choose from different board themes and difficulty levels, and view game history and statistics. Style the layout with a bright red background and a completely transparent interface.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a text-based strategy browser game where players can choose a race, manage resources, upgrade, and battle. The game should have multiple pages, including the base page, ranking page, store, train page, workers page, upgrades page, bank page, messages page, and reports page. Players can choose from four races: Humans, Dragonborns, Elves, and Dwarfs, each with unique attributes. Players can manage resources, including gold, iron, wood, and wheat, and can upgrade their city, workers, and soldiers. Design the site using seashell as the background and crimson for content blocks.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the "Choose Race" page and select each race (Humans, Dragonborns, Elves, Dwarfs) one by one and back to the homepage after selecting for another select.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Each click on a race should display specific attributes unique to that race, and the selection should be confirmed on the page.

evaluation_checklist.question: On the base page, attempt to allocate resources (gold, iron, wood, wheat) to upgrade the city.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The resources should be deducted from the total, and an upgrade confirmation message should appear with visible changes to the city level or attributes.

evaluation_checklist.question: Visit the "Ranking Page" and verify the rank listing of players.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The page should display a sorted list of players by rank, with accurate and clear presentation relevant to their performance metrics.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The selected item should be removed from the store and added to your inventory, with the appropriate deduction in resources.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The upgrade should succeed, show updated soldier stats, and the necessary resources should be spent.

evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The deposited gold should reflect in the bank balance, deducting from available on-hand funds with confirmation of the transaction.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately. Like, one minute it’s sunny, and the next, it’s pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictability, have you ever played those browser games where you get to choose a race? I mean, it’s kind of like picking what to have for dinner, right? Anyway, imagine if you could manage resources like gold and iron while also worrying about what to cook! And then there’s the whole upgrade thing—like upgrading your city or even your workers. It’s like when you try to upgrade your phone but end up just complaining about how the new model isn’t as good as the last one. Oh, and don’t get me started on the neighbors! They’re always talking about their latest battles in those games, but really, who cares? I just want to enjoy my evening with a nice cup of tea. By the way, have you seen those cool game pages? They have all these sections like ranking and store, and it’s all designed with seashell backgrounds and crimson content blocks. But honestly, I’d rather just sit back and watch the clouds roll by while thinking about what races they might be. You know, like Humans, Dragonborns, Elves, and Dwarfs—all that stuff. It’s all so fascinating, but really, I just want to know if it’s going to rain tomorrow!

instruction: Create an immersive narrative-driven adventure where players embark on a journey of self-discovery, selecting their essence from four mystical beings: the wise Humans, the fierce Dragonborns, the graceful Elves, and the sturdy Dwarfs, each radiating their own unique charm. As they navigate through this enchanting realm, players will gather treasures of gold, iron, wood, and wheat, nurturing their kingdom's growth like a gardener tending to a blossoming flower. They will elevate their domain, empowering their loyal companions and valiant warriors, while exploring various realms of knowledge and strategy through pages that unveil the saga of their quest. Envision the backdrop as the gentle whisper of a sandy shore, with the deep hues of a sunset painting the content blocks in a passionate crimson glow, inviting players to dive into their adventure.

instruction: Please implement a text-based strategy browser game where players can choose a race, manage resources, upgrade, and battle. The game should have multiple pages, including the base page, ranking page, store, train page, workers page, upgrades page, bank page, messages page, and reports page. Players can choose from four races: Humans, Dragonborns, Elves, and Dwarfs, each with unique attributes. Players can manage resources, including gold, iron, wood, and wheat, and can upgrade their city, workers, and soldiers. Design the site using a transparent background and bright neon colors for content blocks.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a customer service call logging system to record and manage customer service calls. The system should have functionalities for client identification, call type selection, information recording, action step recording, follow-up step recording, and additional information recording. Users should be able to create new call logs, view open calls, view to-do calls, and generate call log reports. The system should also have a client creator wizard, client information modification functionality, and permission control. Set page background to light pink; color all components with medium violet red.

evaluation_checklist.question: Use the client creator wizard to add a new client.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The new client is saved successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed. The client appears in the client list.

evaluation_checklist.question: Initiate the creation of a new call log and input required fields (e.g., client identification, call type, information recording).
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Call log is created successfully with all entered information preserved, and a confirmation message is displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: Modify an existing client's information.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The changes are saved successfully, a confirmation message is displayed, and updated information is reflected in the client's profile.

evaluation_checklist.question: Select and record an action step in an existing call log.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The action step is recorded and saved successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

evaluation_checklist.question: Check the navigation to view open calls.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: Open calls are displayed in a list format, and the page loads without errors.

evaluation_checklist.question: Access and view the list of to-do calls.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The to-do calls list is displayed correctly and is consistent with previously logged information.

evaluation_checklist.question: Generate a call log report.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The system generates a report containing all relevant call log data, and the report is available for download or viewing.

instruction: You know, I was just thinking about how the weather has been so unpredictable lately, right? Like, one minute it’s sunny and the next it’s pouring rain. Speaking of unpredictable things, have you heard about that new café that opened up downtown? They have the best pastries, but I digress. Anyway, I think it would be a good idea to implement a system that keeps track of all those customer service calls, you know? It should definitely have some way to identify clients, maybe like a little wizard that pops up and helps you out. And oh, you should totally be able to select what type of call it is, like whether it’s a complaint or just someone asking about the weather too! Recording information is key, right? Like, if someone calls about their dinner order being wrong, you want to jot that down. And then, don’t forget about those action steps! Like, maybe you need to follow up with them later, or maybe you just want to complain about your competitors who can’t get it right. Plus, it would be nice to have some way to view open calls and to-do lists, right? Oh, and generating reports could be helpful too, especially if you want to show off how well you’re doing. Just make sure the background is light pink, it’s so calming, and color everything with medium violet red; it’s such a nice combo!

instruction: Create a heartfelt sanctuary for capturing the essence of customer conversations, where each interaction is a cherished memory. This haven should allow for the gentle recognition of clients, the selection of the nature of their inquiries, and the recording of their stories and the actions taken to support them. Envision a space where follow-up intentions bloom and additional insights can be lovingly noted. Users should be able to weave new narratives, explore ongoing dialogues, attend to tasks at hand, and craft reports that reflect the tapestry of interactions. Include a guiding spirit to assist in welcoming new clients, a way to tenderly update their details, and a thoughtful approach to ensure everyone has the right access. Set the ambiance to a soft blush, inviting warmth, and adorn all elements with the vibrant hue of passionate creativity.

ground_truth_instruction: Please implement a web application for an insurance industry automation system to manage insurance business. The system should have functionalities for customer management, policy management, and claims management. Users should be able to browse customer information, policy information, and claims information, and perform corresponding operations. The system should also have data analysis and reporting capabilities, generating various reports and charts. Apply theme using whitesmoke as background and dark cyan for elements.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the customer management section and view the list of customers.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A list of customers is displayed with accurate and relevant information, including customer names, IDs, and contact details.

evaluation_checklist.question: Click on a specific customer's profile to view detailed information.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The detailed customer profile is displayed, showing complete and correct information such as personal details, policy associations, and claim history.

evaluation_checklist.question: Navigate to the policy management section and view the list of insurance policies.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: A list of policies is displayed with accurate information, including policy numbers, types, and status.

evaluation_checklist.question: Select the first policy to view detailed policy information.
evaluation_checklist.expected_answer: The detailed information for the selected policy is displayed correctly, including coverage details, premium amounts, and expiration dates.
